back_translation: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
'Speak to the sons of Israel that they turn back and camp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea; before Baal-zephon, opposite it you shall camp by the sea.'
'Pharaoh will say of the sons of Israel: They are wandering in confusion in the land; the wilderness has shut them in.'
'I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and he will pursue after them; I will be honored through Pharaoh and through all his army, and the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD.' So they did.

It was told to the king of Egypt that the people had fled; the heart of Pharaoh and his servants changed concerning the people, and they said, 'What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?'
He hitched his chariot and took his people with him.
He took 600 chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, with officers over all of them.
The LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the sons of Israel; but the sons of Israel went out with a high hand.
The Egyptians pursued after them and overtook them camped by the sea — all the horses of Pharaoh's chariots, his horsemen, and his army — at Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.
Pharaoh drew near; the sons of Israel lifted up their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians were marching after them. They were very afraid, and the sons of Israel cried out to the LORD.
They said to Moses, 'Was it because there were no graves in Egypt that you took us to die in the wilderness? What is this you have done to us, bringing us out of Egypt?'
'Is this not the word we spoke to you in Egypt, saying, Leave us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians? For it would be better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.'
Moses said to the people, 'Do not fear. Stand firm and see the salvation of the LORD which he will do for you today; for the Egyptians you see today, you shall never see again.'
'The LORD will fight for you, and you shall be silent.'
The LORD said to Moses, 'Why do you cry to me? Speak to the sons of Israel that they go forward.'
'As for you, lift up your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, that the sons of Israel may come in the midst of the sea on dry ground.'

'As for me, behold, I am hardening the heart of the Egyptians, and they shall come in after them; I will be honored through Pharaoh and through all his army, through his chariots, and through his horsemen.'
'The Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD when I am honored through Pharaoh, through his chariots, and through his horsemen.'
The angel of God who went before the camp of Israel moved and went behind them; the pillar of cloud moved from before them and stood behind them.
It came between the camp of Egypt and the camp of Israel. There was the cloud and the darkness, and it lit up the night; one did not come near the other all night.
Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and the LORD drove back the sea with a strong east wind all that night and made the sea into dry land; the waters were divided.
The sons of Israel came in the midst of the sea on dry ground, with the waters as a wall to them on their right and on their left.
The Egyptians pursued and came in after them — every horse of Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen — into the midst of the sea.
In the morning watch, the LORD looked down upon the camp of the Egyptians from the pillar of fire and cloud, and threw the camp of Egypt into confusion.
He removed the wheels of his chariots and made them drive with difficulty; Egypt said, 'Let me flee from before Israel, for the LORD fights for them against Egypt.'
The LORD said to Moses, 'Stretch out your hand over the sea, that the waters may return upon the Egyptians, upon his chariots and upon his horsemen.'
Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and the sea returned at daybreak to its normal flow; the Egyptians were fleeing toward it, and the LORD swept the Egyptians into the midst of the sea.
The waters returned and covered the chariots and the horsemen — all the army of Pharaoh that came after them into the sea; not one of them remained.
But the sons of Israel walked on dry ground in the midst of the sea, with the waters as a wall to them on their right and on their left.
The LORD saved Israel that day from the hand of Egypt; Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the shore of the sea.
Israel saw the great hand which the LORD exercised against Egypt; the people feared the LORD, and believed in the LORD and in Moses his servant.
